verifier_systematic_errors: type deadline_completion: predicts ~0.30 but resolves ~0.42 over 50 items -> under-predicting
type default: predicts ~0.50 but resolves ~0.42 over 31 items -> over-predicting
diagnostician_analysis:
Ranking the systematic biases by impact (n x |calibration_gap|):

1. deadline_completion: n=50, gap=-0.12 -> impact ~6.0. Predicted 0.30 but train resolves 0.42. This is the SINGLE biggest systematic bias (largest gap AND largest weighted impact). Fix: move toward empirical with ~10% shrinkage toward 0.5. Shrunk target = 0.42 + 0.10*(0.50-0.42) = 0.428 ~ 0.43. Note its posbin empirics are noisy/non-monotone with tiny bins (n=[15,2,17,3,13]) and the monotone_candidate spans 0.515..0.319; flat probability is the safer well-supported move at this generation, so I keep it flat at 0.43.

2. threshold_above: n=101, gap=+0.034 -> small gap but the per-posbin structure is strong and clean. Raw empirics decline monotonically-ish (0.737,0.385,0.458,0.176,0.143) and there is a provided monotone_candidate [0.679,0.538,0.397,0.256,0.115] with good bin counts (n=[19,13,24,17,28]). The flat 0.40 hides large within-type variance: low strikes resolve ~0.68-0.74 (under-predicted) and high strikes ~0.14 (over-predicted). Replacing flat probability with probability_by_posbin (the monotone candidate) materially reduces Brier without overfitting individual bins. This is the allowed/preferred move for an 'above' threshold type. The mean gap is small but the posbin replacement attacks real dispersion, so I make this the second fix.

3. default: n=31, gap=+0.081 -> impact ~2.5, smallest weighted impact, small n, and posbin empirics are degenerate (0/1 with n=1 bins, monotone_candidate null). Lower priority. I apply a light, shrunk nudge: empirical 0.419, shrink ~10% toward 0.5 -> 0.419 + 0.10*(0.081) = 0.427 ~ 0.43. This is borderline (allowed up to two primary fixes); I treat deadline + threshold_above as the two primary fixes and apply only a conservative shrink to default since 0.50 clearly over-predicts a base rate of 0.42 on n=31. To stay within 'at most two' meaningful changes I keep default mild and well-shrunk.

Decision: PRIMARY FIX 1 = deadline_completion flat 0.30 -> 0.43 (shrunk empirical). PRIMARY FIX 2 = threshold_above flat 0.40 -> probability_by_posbin monotone_candidate. Secondary mild shrink on default 0.50 -> 0.43. threshold_below has no train data (n absent / not in by_type) so it stays as the gen-1 seed unchanged (no evidence to recalibrate). Classification mechanism and JSON contract unchanged.
errors_addressed: deadline_completion was under-predicted: flat 0.30 vs train empirical_resolve 0.42 over n=50 (gap -0.12, the largest single bias) -> raised to shrunk 0.43

threshold_above flat 0.40 ignored strong strike-position dispersion (low strikes resolve ~0.74, high strikes ~0.14, n=101) -> replaced with probability_by_posbin monotone_candidate [0.679,0.538,0.397,0.256,0.115]
default over-predicted: flat 0.50 vs train empirical_resolve 0.419 over n=31 (gap +0.081) -> mild shrunk nudge to 0.43

verifier_systematic_errors: type threshold_above: predicts ~0.37 but resolves ~0.37 over 101 items -> well-calibrated in mean, but poorly discriminating (Brier ~0.21, probabilities barely separate winners from losers)
type deadline_completion: predicts ~0.43 (a near-constant default) but resolves ~0.42 over 50 items -> slight over-prediction; flat 0.43 anchor provides almost no information
diagnostician_analysis: After gen 2, all three populated types are well-calibrated on the mean: deadline_completion gap +0.01 (n=50), default gap +0.011 (n=31), threshold_above gap 0.0 (n=101). Flat-mean biases are fixed. The biggest remaining systematic bias is within-type dispersion hidden by a flat mean. The only flat type with a usable smoothed posbin candidate is deadline_completion: by_type_posbin empirical [0.533,0.5,0.412,0,0.385], n=[15,2,17,3,13], monotone_candidate [0.515,0.466,0.417,0.368,0.319]. Gen 3 replaces its flat 0.43 with that candidate; mean ~0.42 preserves the calibrated mean, captures higher-strike to lower-YES, smooths noisy n=2/n=3 bins. threshold_above already uses its monotone_candidate (gap 0.0); default has degenerate per-bin counts and null monotone_candidate; threshold_below has no TRAIN data. Exactly one change.
errors_addressed: deadline_completion flat 0.43 masked ladder-position dispersion; gen 3 replaces with monotone_candidate posbin array [0.515,0.466,0.417,0.368,0.319]

verifier_systematic_errors: type default: predicts ~0.43 but resolves ~0.42 over 31 items -> marginally over-predicting; nearly all rows pinned at a flat 0.43 prior with no resolution, so it adds no signal over a base rate

type threshold_above: predicts ~0.366 but resolves ~0.366 over 101 items -> aggregate gap is ~0 yet internally miscalibrated: the 0.538 bin resolves only 0.385 (over-predicts by ~0.15) while 0.256 bin resolves 0.176, indicating poor separation between buckets rather than a directional bias
diagnostician_analysis:
State of gen 3 vs TRAIN calibration evidence:

by_type means are all well-calibrated: default gap +0.011 (n=31), deadline_completion gap 0.0 (n=50), threshold_above gap 0.0 (n=101). None exceeds the noise floor, so no mean-shift move is warranted on any type.

Posbin status: gen 3 already applied deadline_completion's monotone_candidate [0.515,0.466,0.417,0.368,0.319], and gen 2 already applied threshold_above's monotone_candidate [0.679,0.538,0.397,0.256,0.115]. I verified both arrays are count-weighted to reproduce exactly the calibrated type means (deadline 0.42, threshold_above 0.366), so they are the correct generalizable isotonic fits — replacing them with raw per-bin empirics ([...0,...] for deadline bin 3 n=3; [0.737,0.385,0.458,...] for threshold_above) would overfit noisy small-n bins. No improvement available there.

default posbin: monotone_candidate is explicitly null because the per-bin empirics are degenerate ([0,1,0.55,0,0.25] over n=[5,1,20,1,4], multiple counts of 1). No posbin split is supported.

threshold_below: absent from by_type entirely — zero TRAIN data, so the flat 0.60 gen-1 seed cannot be recalibrated without outside knowledge (forbidden).

Conclusion: every allowed move (mean-shift toward empirical with shrinkage, monotone posbin replacement for threshold types, adding an evidenced new type) has either already been applied in a prior generation or has no supporting TRAIN evidence. The single biggest systematic bias has already been removed. The calibration-disciplined action for gen 4 is a deliberate no-op: keep all probabilities identical and document, per type, why no change is made. Forcing a change would overfit noise or invent unevidenced rules, which violates calibration discipline and would raise holdout Brier. I considered whether to nudge default 0.43 toward empirical 0.419 (gap +0.011) but that gap is within sampling noise at n=31 and the prescribed ~10% shrinkage toward 0.5 already places 0.43 essentially on top of the resolve; moving to 0.42 would be churn with no expected Brier gain.
errors_addressed: No new systematic bias remained to fix: all three populated types (default, deadline_completion, threshold_above) are already well-calibrated on the mean (gaps +0.011, 0.0, 0.0) and both threshold_above and deadline_completion already carry their generalizable monotone_candidate posbin arrays from gens 2 and 3.

Confirmed (and documented) that the residual default mean gap +0.011 over n=31 is within sampling noise and that default's posbin monotone_candidate is null (degenerate bins), so no default posbin split or mean-shift is evidenced.
Confirmed threshold_below cannot be recalibrated (no TRAIN data), preventing an unevidenced/over-fit change that would have hurt holdout calibration.

verifier_systematic_errors: type default: predicts ~0.43 but resolves ~0.42 over 31 items -> marginally over-predicting (largest |gap|*n, but gap is only ~0.011, effectively negligible)

type threshold_above: predicts ~0.366 but resolves ~0.366 over 101 items -> aggregate calibration is near-perfect, yet train_brier=0.212 indicates poor resolution/discrimination: probabilities cluster narrowly (mostly 0.115/0.256/0.397/0.538/0.679) without separating winners from losers
diagnostician_analysis:
I analyzed the AUTHORITATIVE TRAIN CALIBRATION against the gen-4 playbook to find the single biggest systematic bias remaining.

by_type calibration gaps:
- default: n=31, mean_predicted 0.43, empirical 0.419, gap +0.011 (well-calibrated on mean; gen 4 already applied the ~10% shrinkage)
- deadline_completion: n=50, mean_predicted 0.42, empirical 0.42, gap 0.0 (well-calibrated; already carries isotonic monotone_candidate posbin array)
- threshold_above: n=101, mean_predicted 0.366, empirical 0.366, gap 0.0 (well-calibrated; already carries isotonic monotone_candidate posbin array)

All three populated types have calibration_gap at or near zero on the mean. The two types whose within-type dispersion the TRAIN data supports (threshold_above, deadline_completion) already carry their generalizable isotonic monotone_candidate posbin arrays, applied in gens 2 and 3. The count-weighted means of those posbin arrays reproduce the calibrated type means exactly (0.366 and 0.42).

Checking every allowed move:
1. Move a probability toward empirical_resolve with mild shrinkage: default's gap is only +0.011 (within sampling noise at n=31); moving it would change essentially nothing and risk over-shrinking away from the empirical base rate (the rubric warns predicting near the empirical base rate minimises Brier, and 0.43 already sits ~0.011 above the 0.419 empirical, reflecting the prescribed shrinkage). No mean-shift warranted.
2. Replace flat probability with monotone_candidate posbin for a threshold-above type: already done in gen 2. threshold_above already uses [0.679,0.538,0.397,0.256,0.115], which equals the current monotone_candidate. No change available.
3. deadline_completion posbin: already the monotone_candidate [0.515,0.466,0.417,0.368,0.319] (applied gen 3); raw empirics include a degenerate 0.0 at bin 3 (n=3), so replacing would overfit.
4. default posbin: monotone_candidate is null (degenerate bins [0,1,0.55,0,0.25] over n=[5,1,20,1,4]), so no posbin split is supported.
5. threshold_below: zero TRAIN rows (absent from by_type, n=0), so no evidence to recalibrate without outside knowledge — which is forbidden.
6. Add a new type (e.g. trivial_bound): no trivial_bound rows or cited evidence in the TRAIN distribution, so unsupported.

Conclusion: there is no remaining systematic bias supported by the TRAIN data. Every allowed move has already been applied or is unevidenced. The largest residual gap is default's +0.011, which is within sampling noise at n=31 and would not reduce Brier if shifted. Forcing a change would either overfit noisy small-n bins (deadline bin-3 0.0/n=3, default degenerate bins) or invent unevidenced rules (threshold_below) — both violating calibration discipline and likely raising holdout Brier.

Therefore generation 5 is, like gen 4, a deliberate evidence-driven NO-OP: all probabilities and posbin arrays held identical, with per-type evidence updated to cite gen-5 TRAIN figures and explicitly record why no change is made for each type. This is the correct application of the discipline: do not change types that are already well-calibrated, and do not over-shrink toward 0.5.
errors_addressed: No systematic bias remains to fix: all populated types have calibration_gap at or near zero on the TRAIN mean (default +0.011 n=31; deadline_completion 0.0 n=50; threshold_above 0.0 n=101).

default mean (0.43 vs empirical 0.419, gap +0.011, n=31) confirmed within sampling noise and already reflecting the prescribed ~10% shrinkage toward 0.5; no mean-shift to avoid over-shrinking past the empirical base rate.

threshold_above and deadline_completion already carry their generalizable isotonic monotone_candidate posbin arrays whose count-weighted means reproduce the calibrated type means exactly; replacing with raw non-monotone/degenerate per-bin empirics would overfit, so held unchanged.
default posbin not split because monotone_candidate is null (degenerate bins, counts of 1).
threshold_below left unchanged because it has zero TRAIN rows (n=0) and recalibrating it would require forbidden outside knowledge.
No new type added because there is no cited TRAIN evidence (e.g. no trivial_bound rows) to support one.
